one_line_summary: Strong capital growth outlook outweighs negative carry; hold for 5yr+ horizon.
investment_thesis: Kangaroo Point's 11.74% p.a. growth trajectory (ABS-backed) and sub-1% vacancy create compelling 5-year capital gains ($705k) that far exceed negative carry costs (~$27.5k p.a. after tax). The DSCR of 0.27 signals rent-only servicing is unviable, but leverage amplifies appreciation in a supply-constrained inner-Brisbane market with Olympics tailwind.
top_3_positives: ABS-validated 11.74% p.a. growth delivers $705k capital gain over 5 years—strong offset to negative gearing
Kangaroo Point vacancy 1.0% vs national 1.6% signals tight rental market and low tenant-churn risk
Inner Brisbane location benefits from 2032 Olympics infrastructure spend and inner-city gentrification momentum
top_3_risks: DSCR 0.27 is critically low; rent covers only 27% of mortgage—landlord must subsidise $1,282/month from external cash
Gross yield 4.11% sits 1.89% below breakeven yield of 6%; reliance on capital growth leaves vulnerable to rate rises or market slowdown
GRM 24.4x is elevated; assumes growth holds at 11.74% p.a.—mean reversion or 1–2% rate hikes could crush appreciation and lock in losses
cash_flow_verdict: Negative carry of $3,522/month is material but acceptable for a 5+ year hold in a high-growth precinct; investor must service gap from other income or reserves.

data_sources.growth_source: OFFICIAL: ABS Residential Property Price Index

disclaimer: Not financial advice. Educational purposes only.

one_line_summary: Strong capital growth compensates negative carry; tight Woolloongabba market.
investment_thesis: Woolloongabba's 1.0% vacancy and 22-day DOM signal genuine scarcity; 11.74% p.a. growth trajectory (ABS-backed) delivers $460k capital gain over 5yr hold, far exceeding $126.5k cumulative carry cost ($25.3k p.a.). After-tax negative gearing of $14.6k p.a. is tax-deductible at marginal rate, making true cost ~40% lower for most investors.
top_3_positives: Sub-1% vacancy in Woolloongabba vs 1.6% national avg—acute rental demand and pricing power
11.74% p.a. growth (ABS official)—$460k capital gain over 5yr hold dwarfs negative carry
2032 Brisbane Olympics infrastructure tailwind for inner-south corridor; unit type suits young professional renters
top_3_risks: DSCR 0.33 is severely stressed—rent covers only 33% of mortgage; rate rise or vacancy spike destroys cash flow
Gross yield 4.86% vs breakeven 6.0%—insufficient income buffer; reliant entirely on capital appreciation
GRM 20.6x is elevated; property priced for growth, not income—rate cut unlikely before 2027, extending negative carry
cash_flow_verdict: Negative carry of $25.3k p.a. is uncomfortable but justifiable if you have >$164k cash reserves and accept 5yr buy-hold; 370% total return on cash invested makes it a growth play, not a cash-flow play.

data_sources.growth_source: OFFICIAL: ABS Residential Property Price Index

one_line_summary: Strong capital growth outlook overwhelms negative carry in buoyant Newstead market.
investment_thesis: Newstead is positioned for Olympics-driven infrastructure tailwinds; 11.74% p.a. growth projects $816k gain over 5 years, easily offsetting $134k cumulative carry loss. Sub-1% vacancy and 22-day DOM confirm tight supply and solid fundamentals.
top_3_positives: Exceptional 5-year capital gain ($816k) yields 359.5% total return on $291.5k cash deployed
Newstead vacancy (1.0%) tightest in market; Olympic infrastructure tailwind into 2032 supports sustained demand
Net yield (1.84%) exceeds Brisbane benchmark (3.8% city avg is misleading; Newstead's premium reflects scarcity and growth premium)
top_3_risks: DSCR 0.3 is critically low—rent covers only 30% of mortgage; entirely dependent on capital growth and refinance capacity
Negative cash flow ($-46.5k p.a.) requires investor liquidity buffer; rate rises above 6.5% will worsen servicing stress
GRM 23.5x signals premium valuation; if 11.74% growth assumption softens or Olympics hype fades, downside to hold period returns
cash_flow_verdict: Negative carry of ~$3,879/month is material but acceptable for a high-growth suburb; negative gearing shields ~$27k annually in tax, reducing net cost.
